title: Empty arrays and collections should be returned instead of null
description: Empty arrays and collections should be returned instead of nullAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?Returningnullordefaultinstead of an actual collection forces the method callers to explicitly test for null, making the code more complex and less readable.Moreover, in many cases,nullordefaultis used as a synonym for empty.Noncompliant code examplepublic Result[] GetResults() { return null; // Noncompliant } public IEnumerable<Result> GetResults(bool condition) { var results = GenerateResults(); return condition ? results : null; // Noncompliant } public IEnumerable<Result> GetResults() => null; // Noncompliant public IEnumerable<Result> Results { get { return default(IEnumerable<Result>); // Noncompliant } } public IEnumerable<Result> Results =
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

logic.detection_conditions: Methods or properties with collection/array return types that return null or default
logic.triggering_conditions: (return_type contains collection/array type) AND (contains return null OR return default OR => null)
logic.never_trigger_on: Not applicable - rule targets specific return statements in methods/properties with collection return types
logic.reasoning: Detects methods/properties returning collections or arrays that return null or default instead of empty collections. This forces callers to check for null unnecessarily. AST-based approach identifies MethodDeclaration and PropertyDeclaration nodes, checks return type for collection patterns (IEnumerable, List, Array with [], Collection, etc.), and scans source for 'return null', 'return default', or '=> null' patterns. Excludes comments where return null appears.

should_trigger: public IEnumerable<Result> Results { get { return default(IEnumerable<Result>); } }
should_not_trigger: public Result[] GetResults() { return new Result[0]; }
public IEnumerable<Result> Results { get { return Enumerable.Empty<Result>(); } }
public List<string> GetItems() { return new List<string>(); }

checks.message: Return empty collection instead of null
